sources.verification_method: Cockroach Labs official 'About' page documents the company's founding in 2015 by Spencer Kimball, Peter Mattis, and Ben Darnell (former Google engineers). No rebranding or refounding events recorded for the Cockroach Labs Inc. legal entity. Cross-confirmed by Bloomberg company profile (entity ID 1284590D:US) and PitchBook 2026 company profile.

Cockroach Labs official Contact page lists the principal U.S. address as 125 West 25th Street, 11th Floor, New York, NY 10001. Cross-confirmed by ZoomInfo (entity ID 369384184) and LeadIQ company profile. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code: US.

Principal place of business: 125 West 25th Street, 11th Floor, New York, NY 10001. Atlas convention per METHODOLOGY 7.2 uses canonical short form 'New York' (no 'New York City' redundancy). Distributed offices in Austin, Bangalore, London, Toronto are documented (Built In NYC, ZoomInfo) but do not change the legal HQ designation.

Series F (2021-12-16) is the most recent disclosed funding round. The schema enum 'series-e-plus' is the catch-all for Series E, F, G+. No Series G or later round announced through 2026-05-05 (verified against cockroachlabs.com/press, latest release 2026-01-14, and cross-confirmed by absence in TechCrunch / PRNewswire / Crunchbase / PitchBook / Tracxn).

PitchBook 2026 Cockroach Labs company profile reports aggregate total raised of USD 633M across all disclosed funding rounds (Series A through F, plus undisclosed seed). Manual sum of Series A-F amounts: USD 626.6M (A=20M, B=27M, C=55M, D=86.6M, E=160M, F=278M). Implied undisclosed seed/pre-A: ~USD 6.4M to reconcile with aggregate. Per METHODOLOGY 7.4 private-company convention.

Tracxn 2026 Cockroach Labs company profile independently reports USD 633M total raised, consistent with the PitchBook 2026 aggregate. Forge Global pre-IPO page (2023) and Crunchbase organization page also report USD 633M, providing 4 independent database sources converging on the same figure. NOTE: source.type='other' used because schema v1.0 lacks a 'tracxn' or 'fundraising-database' enum (see schema TODO section 8).

Cockroach Labs official press release dated 2021-12-16 announces the Series F at USD 278M raised on USD 5B post-money valuation, led by Greenoaks. This is the company's most recent disclosed funding round; no subsequent round announced through 2026-05-05. Per METHODOLOGY 7.5 private-company convention, last_valuation_usd captures the post-money declared in the most recent disclosed round.

TechCrunch article by Ron Miller, published 2021-12-16 contemporaneous with Cockroach Labs Series F announcement. Independently reports USD 278M Series F at USD 5B post-money. Cross-confirmation of company press release per METHODOLOGY 3.2 (≥2 independent sources for high-stakes claims). Additional independent corroboration: CNBC 2021/12/16, PRNewswire 301445993, PitchBook 2026 profile.

Forge Global pre-IPO investment page for Cockroach Labs confirms private status as of 2026-05-05. No SEC filings exist for Cockroach Labs Inc. (no S-1, no 10-K). Cockroach Labs press page (cockroachlabs.com/press, latest release dated 2026-01-14) makes no IPO announcement. NOTE: source.type='other' used because Forge Global is a private-market data provider not currently in the schema enum (see schema TODO section 8).

license_history.rationale_public: Per Cockroach Labs' 2019 relicensing announcement: the relicense was driven by the free-rider problem of cloud providers offering CockroachDB-as-a-service without contributing back. BSL-1.1 forbids commercial hosted offerings without a paid license while remaining free for community/internal use; code automatically converts to Apache-2.0 three years after each release.

Per Cockroach Labs' August 2024 announcement: the prior open-core model created two issues — startups wanted Enterprise features but were unwilling/unable to pay for the full Enterprise license, and larger enterprises were sticking with the free Core tier even when Enterprise made business sense. The new model uses a single CSL Enterprise license with a free tier for entities under USD 10M annual revenue and paid per-CPU pricing above that threshold. Effective for releases v24.3 onward starting 2024-11-18.

sources.verification_method: Git commit c0274df ('license: change to the new CockroachDB Software License', authored 2024-09-26 16:26:59 -0400 EDT by James H. Linder, internal ticket RE-658) replaces the prior multi-license LICENSE file (BSL-1.1 + CCL + MIT + BSD-style mixed) with the full CockroachDB Software License (CSL) text. README.md updated in the same commit to state: 'All versions released after November 18, 2024, including patch fixes for prior versions 23.1 onward, are published under the CockroachDB Software License (CSL).' Atlas canonical short form: CSL (no explicit version in LICENSE file, analog to SUL convention per METHODOLOGY 2.4).

Live LICENSE file at master HEAD of cockroachdb/cockroach as of 2026-05-05 contains the full CSL text (License Key requirement, mandatory Telemetry, Limited Non-Production Use definition, USD 100 liability cap). Confirms CSL remains the current canonical license; no further license change has occurred between commit c0274df (2024-09-26) and 2026-05-05.

Cockroach Labs official 2019 relicensing announcement (Spencer Kimball) implicitly confirms Apache-2.0 as the original license being departed from ('Why We're Relicensing CockroachDB'). Cross-confirmed by RedMonk analyst writeup (redmonk.com/sogrady/2019/06/21/cockroach-source-available/) and by tech press coverage (SD Times, Open Source for U) which explicitly state CockroachDB started under Apache-2.0 prior to the BSL-1.1 transition.

Source for the Apache-2.0 → BSL-1.1 license_history entry (year=2019, month=6). Per METHODOLOGY 7.7 source hierarchy item (1): direct official statement from the company. NOTE: exact GitHub commit that physically changed LICENSE.txt in cockroachdb/cockroach for the 2019 transition was not extracted during data collection (web_fetch hit token limits paginating commits view); pending git archaeology pass. Per METHODOLOGY 7.8, commit URL is the strongest primary source but not strictly required when company blog is canonical.

Source for the BSL-1.1 → CSL license_history entry (year=2024, month=9). Per METHODOLOGY 7.8: the commit that physically replaces LICENSE files is the strongest primary source. Commit c0274df is the canonical artifact: it deletes the prior 5-line multi-license disclaimer and inserts the full 166-line CSL text, simultaneously updating README.md to declare effective release date 2024-11-18.

Secondary corroboration of BSL-1.1 → CSL transition rationale. TechCrunch article (Ron Miller, 2024-08-15) reports the announcement contemporaneously and quotes CEO Spencer Kimball's stated motivation: the Core free tier had become a 'savviest competitor' to Enterprise; new model uses USD 10M revenue threshold for free Enterprise tier, per-CPU pricing above. Independent of the commit primary source per METHODOLOGY 3.2.

Additional secondary corroboration of the 2024 transition (BigDATAwire, 2024-08-19). Independent of TechCrunch, both reporting on the same announcement window (2024-08-15) but each providing corroborating quotes from Cockroach Labs leadership. Used per METHODOLOGY 7.7 source hierarchy item (2): verbatim quote of the company in reputable tech press contemporaneous with the announcement.

Current LICENSE file (CSL) is a single license covering all CockroachDB code; no clause offers an alternative concurrent license that the user can elect. Therefore dual_license=false per METHODOLOGY 7.9 strict definition ('same source code offered under two concurrent licenses, with the user choosing which one applies'). Pre-2024 state was heterogeneous (different files under different licenses: BSL-1.1, CCL, MIT, BSD-style) — also not dual licensing per 7.9 (heterogeneous repos are captured in metadata.notes, not via dual_license). Documented in metadata.notes.

CockroachDB uses CLA Assistant bot integration at cla-assistant.io/cockroachdb/cockroach. Per METHODOLOGY 7.10 hierarchy item (2): 'Visible CLA bot integration on PRs (CLA Assistant, EasyCLA, custom GitHub Action) that blocks merge until signed.' This is the canonical mechanism for cla_required=true. Cross-referenced by HN discussion (news.ycombinator.com/item?id=20097077) and a public CLA gist by founder Ben Darnell (gist.github.com/bdarnell/7709a24b98a9914f2920) confirming long-standing CLA practice.

Sacra private-market research profile for Cockroach Labs identifies multiple parallel commercial offerings as of 2025-2026: (1) CockroachDB Cloud (managed service with Standard variable and Advanced fixed tiers); (2) Self-hosted CockroachDB under the CockroachDB Software License (CSL), free for entities with annual revenue under USD 10M and paid per-CPU above that threshold (post-2024 license change). Three+ material parallel offerings places CockroachDB closest to the 'hybrid' enum per METHODOLOGY 2.1. Edge-case caveat: post-2024 CockroachDB lacks a classic OSS layer (CSL is source-available proprietary, not OSI-open), so the strict 2.1 'hybrid' definition fails on the 'OSS' leg. Atlas convention: pick the least-bad existing bucket and document the strict-fit failure in metadata.notes (see methodology TODO section 8 on the proposed v1.1 'source-available-tiered' enum).

Cockroach Labs official press release (via PRNewswire) confirms the existence of multiple Cloud tier offerings ('Enhanced Cloud Tiers') alongside the self-hosted Enterprise model. Independent corroboration that the company itself markets multiple parallel commercial paths consistent with a 'hybrid' classification.

Best available third-party estimate (Sacra private-market research) indicates that as of late 2021 / early 2022, most of Cockroach Labs' revenue came from self-hosted customers, with CockroachDB Cloud representing approximately 50% of customer count but a smaller revenue share, while growing materially faster (cited 500% QoQ Cloud growth in Q4 2021). This is reported as a third-party analyst estimate, NOT a company-disclosed fact — Cockroach Labs has not publicly disclosed its revenue mix. The post-2024 CSL transition routes ALL self-hosted users (including the Free tier under USD 10M) through a centralized License Key model, which structurally reinforces the enterprise-license revenue stream channel rather than shifting to Cloud. Confidence: MEDIUM (single private-market research source, no SEC corroboration possible). 'enterprise-license' enum applied per schema commercial_model.primary_revenue_stream; alternative 'cloud' would have been defensible if Cloud were dominant per the 2021 trajectory but the third-party estimate places self-hosted as majority.

Cockroach Labs official press release dated 2026-01-14 ('Cockroach Labs Accelerates Momentum into 2026') emphasizes enterprise customer rebuilds 'for AI-Scale Resilience' as the core narrative, consistent with self-hosted enterprise licensing being a material revenue stream. The October 2025 IBM OEM partnership (CockroachDB on LinuxONE, IBM Z, Power Systems, Red Hat OpenShift, IBM Cloud) is a self-hosted enterprise-license channel deal that further reinforces the inference. Independent corroboration of the Sacra private-market estimate.

pricing.free_tier_notes: Two distinct free tiers exist as of 2026-05-05: (1) CockroachDB Cloud Basic (managed): USD 0/month, 50 million Request Units (RUs) and 10 GiB storage free per month, on-demand compute scales to zero, AWS and GCP regions, 99.99% availability, suitable for smaller bursty workloads up to 30K RU/sec (~60 vCPUs equivalent); (2) Self-hosted CSL Free (post-2024 model): the full Enterprise feature set is free for entities with annual revenue under USD 10M (self-attested), but requires a License Key from Cockroach Labs and mandatory Telemetry sharing, with clusters throttling after 7 days without telemetry. License must be renewed annually.
pricing.self_hosted_restrictions: Self-hosted CockroachDB under the CockroachDB Software License (CSL, post-2024-09-26 commit c0274df, effective with v24.3 release on 2024-11-18) is free for entities with annual revenue under USD 10M (self-attested), but is NOT classic OSS. Restrictions documented in the LICENSE file at github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/LICENSE: (a) License Key required from Cockroach Labs to operate beyond Limited Non-Production Use (where Limited Non-Production Use means design/prototyping/testing/development in non-production environments OR a single-node deployment); (b) mandatory Telemetry sharing to Cockroach Labs unless an Enterprise Trial / Evaluation License Key explicitly permits disabling it; (c) clusters are throttled after 7 days without sending Telemetry; (d) prohibition on publishing Benchmark results without permitted right (mutual benchmark restriction); (e) Protective Measures (License Key checks, Telemetry mechanisms) cannot be circumvented or modified; (f) license must be renewed annually or as negotiated. Entities with annual revenue at or above USD 10M require a paid Enterprise CSL license, priced sales-led (no public per-CPU rate disclosed).

sources.verification_method: CockroachDB pricing page lists 'Basic — Starts at $0 / month' as the leftmost tier with the description 'For smaller, bursty workloads up to 30K RU/sec (~60 vCPUs)' and includes '50 million RUs and 10 GiB storage free per month' and 'On-demand compute and storage that scales to zero'. Confirms Cloud Basic is a free tier with no payment information required.

CockroachDB Licensing FAQs documents the post-2024 self-hosted CSL Free tier: full Enterprise feature set free for entities with annual revenue under USD 10M (self-attested). Independent of the Cloud Basic free tier; both free options exist concurrently.

CSL LICENSE text (extracted via commit c0274df.patch on objects.githubusercontent.com on 2026-05-05): Limited Non-Production Use is permitted indefinitely without a License Key (single-node, non-production deployments). Beyond that, the CSL Free license is renewable annually for entities under USD 10M revenue; there is no time-limited expiration for qualifying entities, though the conditions (License Key, Telemetry, revenue threshold) are non-trivial. self_hosted_free_indefinite=true is correct in the dataset semantic ('free for some users indefinitely'); restrictions captured in self_hosted_restrictions.

CockroachDB pricing page (browser-validated by Pablo on 2026-05-05) displays three tiers: Basic ($0/month, free, excluded per METHODOLOGY 7.17), Standard PREVIEW ('2 vCPUs start at $0.18 / hr', excluded per METHODOLOGY 7.17 as non-GA PREVIEW status), Advanced ('4 vCPUs start at $0.60 / hr', the lowest fixed-price GA paid tier, captured). Conversion: $0.60/hr cluster total × 730 hours/month convention = USD 438.00/month. The price reflects base compute only; storage, network egress, and add-ons (CMEK, multi-region cross-region transfer, vector data integration, AI capabilities) are billed separately as additional usage charges. Wording interpretation: 'X vCPUs start at $Y/hr' read as cluster-total pricing per 7.17 example; per-vCPU interpretation rejected after sanity check (per-vCPU premium between Standard and Advanced under the cluster-total reading is 67%, which aligns with feature differential; per-vCPU reading would imply 233% premium which is implausible).

CockroachDB pricing combines multiple obligatory dimensions: (1) compute capacity (vCPU tier — Basic/Standard/Advanced), (2) storage usage (per GB — on-demand for Basic and Standard, provisioned for Advanced), (3) network egress (per GB), (4) add-on services (vector data integration, AI capabilities, observability integrations). Multi-dimensional canonical pricing structure → 'hybrid' enum per METHODOLOGY 7.11 (≥2 mandatory or recurring dimensions in the canonical bill structure).

CockroachDB Licensing FAQs documents that paid Enterprise CSL licensing for entities with annual revenue at or above USD 10M is sales-led ('License must be renewed annually or as negotiated') with no per-CPU rate publicly disclosed. Cross-confirmed by absence of self-serve Enterprise pricing on cockroachlabs.com/pricing as of 2026-05-05. Set to null per Atlas convention; sales-led pricing is common for enterprise databases.

feature_gating.notes: CockroachDB gates canonical enterprise features (SSO/SAML, fine-grained RBAC, audit logs, SLA support, multi-region HA) behind a commercial relationship/license mechanism. Advanced analytics is partial: basic SQL/time-series querying is available, while CDC/changefeeds and advanced operational data features require Enterprise/License Key access. White-label and API rate limits are not applicable to a database engine product.

sources.verification_method: CockroachDB Manual page on SSO for DB Console documents that SAML and OIDC single-sign-on for the self-hosted DB Console requires a valid CockroachDB Enterprise license. Cross-confirmed by Cloud Organization SSO docs (cockroachlabs.com/docs/cockroachcloud/cloud-org-sso): SAML and OpenID Connect identity providers are configurable for CockroachDB Cloud organizations, with Cloud deployments automatically having a valid Enterprise license. Atlas value 'commercial' applied: SSO requires either CSL Free or CSL Paid License Key for self-hosted, or Cloud subscription. Not available without a commercial relationship with Cockroach Labs.

CockroachDB Authorization docs document basic built-in roles (admin, root, user, sql_admin) available in all deployments and a granular fine-grained role/privilege system with custom roles, granular grants (e.g., on tables, schemas, sequences), and inheritance available with Enterprise license. The field 'rbac_fine_grained' specifically measures fine-grained RBAC capability, not the existence of any RBAC: post-2024 CSL Free + Paid both grant fine-grained custom roles via License Key. Atlas value 'commercial' applied (consistent with MongoDB precedent: field measures fine-grained capability, not basic-RBAC presence).

CockroachDB SQL Audit Logging documentation describes role-based audit logging via the `sql.log.user_audit` cluster setting and the `role_based_audit_event` event type. The feature is gated behind Enterprise license — confirmed by CockroachDB Feature Availability page and Enterprise Features index. Auditing capability is not in the LNPU (Limited Non-Production Use) carve-out; requires License Key (CSL Free for ≤USD 10M revenue, or CSL Paid). Atlas value 'commercial'.

Cockroach Labs offers tiered support coupled to commercial relationship: (1) Community-only (forum, Slack, no SLA) for self-hosted CSL Free users; (2) Standard support with SLA-backed response times for paid CSL and Cloud subscribers; (3) Premium support tier for Cloud Advanced subscribers (99.999% multi-region availability SLA per pricing page). Cloud Basic tier (free) gets 99.99% availability SLA but with self-service support. Tier-based SLA-backed support is therefore a commercial feature (not present in LNPU/CSL Free non-paid tiers without an explicit support contract).

Licensing FAQs confirm the support-tier model: CSL Free users (≤USD 10M revenue) receive community support only — Docs, Forum, Slack — without contractual SLA. Cross-confirmation of pricing-page SLA tier structure.

CockroachDB Multi-Region Capabilities Overview documents that built-in 3x Raft replication is single-region (or single-cluster) in all deployments — that is the default distributed architecture, not the multi-region HA feature being measured. The 'multi_region_ha' field specifically captures multi-region clusters with cross-region survival goals (REGION_FAILURE, ZONE_FAILURE) and automated cross-region failover orchestration; these capabilities require Enterprise license (License Key gate). Atlas value 'commercial' applied (consistent with MongoDB precedent: field measures cross-region capability, not basic in-region replication).

CockroachDB is a database engine; white-label / OEM rebranding is not a user-facing product feature that customers can enable through tier selection. The October 2025 IBM OEM partnership ('CockroachDB PostgreSQL for IBM') referenced is a B2B negotiated partnership deal, not a self-serve product capability. Marked 'na' (not applicable to product type) per Atlas methodology, consistent with MongoDB precedent.

Change Data Capture (CDC) Overview documents that Enterprise Changefeeds with Kafka sinks, cloud-storage sinks, advanced filtering, and guaranteed delivery semantics require an Enterprise license (License Key gate). Basic time-series queries are part of core SQL and available in all deployments including LNPU. Vector search (pgvector + C-SPANN indexing) per cockroachlabs.com/blog/vector-search-pgvector-cockroachdb/ is available across Cloud tiers; self-hosted availability via CSL Free unclear but likely included. External BI integrations (Tableau, Looker, Grafana) are user-side, not gated by CockroachDB. Atlas value 'partial' applied: basic time-series SQL = OSS-equivalent (available without License Key in LNPU); CDC + advanced operational data features = commercial. Consistent with MongoDB precedent (aggregation framework OSS + BI/Charts/Search commercial = partial).

CockroachDB is accessed via the PostgreSQL wire protocol (SQL queries over TCP), not via a HTTP/REST API service. There is no user-facing 'API rate limit' feature applicable to the data plane. Cloud Admin API (cluster management endpoints) has operational rate limits but those are not the user-facing data API rate limits the field is intended to measure. Marked 'na' (not applicable to product type), consistent with MongoDB precedent.

metadata.notes: Phase 1-6 complete as of 2026-05-05 (data collection date). Phase 7 pending. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 1: (1) HQ disambiguation: Cockroach Labs maintains a legal HQ in New York (125 W 25th St, 11th Floor, NY 10001) and additional offices in Austin, Bangalore, London, Toronto. Despite remote-friendly hiring posture, the legal HQ and principal place of business is unchanged. Per METHODOLOGY 7.2, hq_city='New York' (short form, no 'City' suffix). (2) total_raised_usd derivation per METHODOLOGY 7.4 (private-company variant): aggregate of USD 633M reported by 4 independent DBs (PitchBook 2026, Tracxn 2026, Crunchbase, Forge Global 2023). Manual sum of disclosed Series A-F: USD 626.6M (A=20M, B=27M, C=55M, D=86.6M, E=160M, F=278M). Implied undisclosed seed/pre-A round: ~USD 6.4M to reconcile to the aggregate. Seed amount not publicly disclosed. (3) last_valuation_usd=USD 5B reflects post-money declared in Series F (2021-12-16). Per METHODOLOGY 7.5 private-company convention. Caveat: snapshot is ~4.4 years old at data_collection_date (2026-05-05); SaaS valuation reset 2022-2023 may have impacted real fair value, but no public down-round, secondary tender, or revaluation event has been reported for Cockroach Labs in primary sources. The 5B figure should be interpreted as 'most recent disclosed post-money', not 'current fair value'. (4) 'Series G' signal rejected: salestools.io/en/report/cockroach-labs-raises-278m-series-g claimed a Series G of USD 278M at USD 5B post-money in October 2025. Rejected because: (a) cifras identical to Series F (Dec 2021) — Series G would be USD 278M+, USD 5B+, and likely have a different lead investor mix, not the exact same numbers; (b) zero corroboration in cockroachlabs.com/press (latest release 2026-01-14, no Series G mention), TechCrunch, PRNewswire, CNBC, Crunchbase, PitchBook, Tracxn; (c) total_raised_usd aggregate across 4 DBs unchanged at USD 633M — a real Series G of USD 278M would push aggregate to ~USD 911M, which no source reports. Conclusion: salestools.io entry is a parser error (incremented round letter without verification). Pablo validated this conclusion via direct browser checks of cockroachlabs.com/press, /news/press-release-series-f-funding/, and /about/ on 2026-05-05. (5) Outlier rejected: Startup Intros (Apr 2026) reports USD 801.3M total raised across '9 rounds'. Discrepancy of USD 168M with the 4-DB consensus of USD 633M. No corroboration in primary sources. Rejected as outlier. (6) Series E date corrected from initial agent output: agent reported 'May 2021' for Series E; cross-validation against TechCrunch 2021/01/12, cockroachlabs.com/blog/explosive-growth-2021/, and PRNewswire 301205778 confirms 2021-01-12. Series E (Jan 2021) followed by Series F (Dec 2021) — same year — is consistent with the 'doubled valuation in less than 12 months' narrative in TechCrunch's Series F coverage. (7) Source type 'other' used for Tracxn and Forge Global pending schema TODO (section 8) to add 'fundraising-database' or 'private-market-data' enum value. (8) Pablo accepted the proposed Phase 1 values on 2026-05-05 after reviewing the agent + WebSearch cross-validation report (4-DB consensus on USD 633M total raised; Series F primary source verified independently in cockroachlabs.com press release + TechCrunch + CNBC + PRNewswire + PitchBook + Crunchbase). Browser-side per-URL validation by Pablo not explicitly performed for each of the 3 suggested checks (cockroachlabs.com/press, /news/press-release-series-f-funding/, /about/) — high-stakes claims (total_raised_usd, last_valuation_usd) instead validated via independent direct WebSearch (not delegated to sub-agent), per memory rule 'Validate agent research with direct WebSearch'. Per Atlas convention any reader can verify each claim against the listed source URLs. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 2: (9) Current license is CSL (CockroachDB Software License) as of commit c0274df57a9f8d0086577bcf74c81110db2cea22 (authored 2024-09-26 16:26:59 -0400 EDT by James H. Linder, Cockroach Labs internal ticket RE-658). Same commit updated README.md to declare 'All versions released after November 18, 2024, including patch fixes for prior versions 23.1 onward, are published under the CockroachDB Software License (CSL)' — so three dates exist for this transition: public announcement (2024-08-15), LICENSE file change in repo (2024-09-26), effective for releases (2024-11-18, v24.3). Per METHODOLOGY 7.8, license_history uses the commit date (2024-09-26 → year=2024, month=9). All three dates fall within the L24M window (cutoff 2024-05-04) for strategic_signals.license_changes_l24m, to be populated in Phase 7 with the same commit URL as canonical source. (10) The 2019 Apache→BSL transition source uses the official Cockroach Labs blog (cockroachlabs.com/blog/oss-relicensing-cockroachdb/) per METHODOLOGY 7.7 hierarchy item (1) — direct official statement from the company. The exact GitHub commit that physically replaced LICENSE.txt in cockroachdb/cockroach during the 2019 transition was NOT extracted during this data collection: web_fetch attempts on github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/commits/master/LICENSE returned content exceeding token limits, and the temporary file path was not accessible from the bash sandbox for grep-based extraction. Per METHODOLOGY 7.8, commit URL is the 'strongest primary source' but explicitly acknowledged as ideal-not-required when the company blog is the canonical announcement. Open TODO: extract 2019 commit hash via git archaeology (e.g., 'git -C /path/to/cockroach log --follow --oneline LICENSE.txt' on a local clone) and add as additional source on Day-2 sanity check. (11) dual_license=false. Pre-2024 CockroachDB had a HETEROGENEOUS repo: per the LICENSE file diff in commit c0274df, the prior state declared source code 'variously licensed under the Business Source License 1.1 (BSL), the CockroachDB Community License (CCL), the MIT license, BSD-style licenses, and other licenses specified in the source code' — different files under different licenses, NOT the same code under two concurrent licenses. Per METHODOLOGY 7.9: heterogeneous repos are captured in metadata.notes (this note), not via dual_license. Post-2024 CockroachDB ships under a single CSL covering all code: trivially not dual licensing (no alternative concurrent license offered). Both states fail the strict 7.9 definition. (12) cla_required=true via CLA Assistant bot integration at cla-assistant.io/cockroachdb/cockroach. Per METHODOLOGY 7.10 hierarchy item (2): visible CLA bot blocks PR merge until signed. Long-standing practice (founder Ben Darnell published a CLA gist in 2014 at gist.github.com/bdarnell/7709a24b98a9914f2920); confirmed still active in 2026-05-05 PRs. Stronger than DCO-only sign-off, weaker than out-of-band company legal page (MongoDB's model). (13) Atlas canonical short form 'CSL' added to METHODOLOGY 2.4 vocabulary as part of Phase 2: the license file at master HEAD does not specify an explicit version number ('CockroachDB Software License' with no v1.0 or similar), so the convention follows the SUL precedent (n8n's Sustainable Use License — non-standard, no version). If Cockroach Labs ever publishes CSL-2.0, the canonical short form would become CSL-2.0 and 2.4 vocabulary would be amended. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 3: (14) commercial_model.type='hybrid' is a least-bad fit for CockroachDB post-2024 CSL transition. The strict METHODOLOGY 2.1 definition of 'hybrid' (and of every other enum: pure-open-core, hosted-first, services-support, saas-with-oss-marketing) explicitly assumes the existence of an OSS layer. Post-2024 CockroachDB has NO OSS layer — CSL is source-available proprietary (License Key required, Telemetry mandatory; not OSI-open). However, CockroachDB combines source-available self-hosted licensing (CSL), free use under a USD 10M annual revenue threshold, paid self-hosted Enterprise licensing, and CockroachDB Cloud (Standard variable + Advanced fixed tiers) — three or more material parallel offerings. CockroachDB is classified as 'hybrid' in Atlas v1 as a least-bad fit; it is not classic OSS open-core after the 2024 CSL transition. The case is preserved in the Atlas Free Preview because it is a central pedagogical example of the license migration pattern (OSS/permissive → BSL/source-available → proprietary source-available + cloud) that the dataset is designed to test (Hypothesis 1). Schema TODO: METHODOLOGY 8 records a proposed v1.1 enum value 'source-available-tiered' to capture this pattern more precisely without forcing the 'hybrid' fit. (15) commercial_model.primary_revenue_stream='enterprise-license' is presented as MEDIUM-confidence third-party estimate, NOT as company-disclosed fact. Cockroach Labs is private and has never publicly disclosed its revenue mix between Cloud and self-hosted Enterprise. The best available source is Sacra private-market research (sacra.com/c/cockroach-labs/), which states that most of the company's revenue comes from self-hosted customers, while Cloud represents approximately 50% of customer count but a smaller revenue share — Cloud is a major growth channel but not the primary revenue line. Independent corroboration: the October 2025 IBM OEM partnership (deploying CockroachDB on LinuxONE, IBM Z, Power Systems, Red Hat OpenShift, IBM Cloud) is a self-hosted enterprise-license channel deal of meaningful scale; the post-2024 CSL transition routes ALL self-hosted users (including the Free ≤USD 10M tier) through centralized License Key issuance, structurally reinforcing the enterprise-license channel. Wording is conservative: 'best available third-party estimate indicates self-hosted/enterprise-license revenue remains the majority, while Cloud is a major growth channel' — not 'company has stated' or 'verified by SEC filing'. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 4: (16) CockroachDB Cloud Standard tier (starting at USD 0.18/hr for 2 vCPUs cluster total = USD 131.40/month) is currently labeled PREVIEW and is excluded from cloud_entry_price_usd_month per METHODOLOGY 7.17 amendment added on 2026-05-05 ('Products or tiers labeled as PREVIEW, BETA, or similar non-GA status are excluded from cloud_entry_price_usd_month, unless they are the only paid public cloud option available. Atlas captures stable, generally available pricing as canonical.'). Atlas captures the lowest stable GA paid tier, which is Cloud Advanced (4 vCPUs at USD 0.60/hr cluster total = USD 438/month). Pricing reflects base compute only; storage, egress, and add-ons (CMEK, vector data integration, AI capabilities, multi-region transfer) are billed separately as additional usage charges. Wording interpretation of 'X vCPUs start at $Y/hr' resolved as cluster-total pricing (not per-vCPU) based on natural English reading + sanity check: per-vCPU premium between Standard ($0.09/vCPU-hr cluster-implied) and Advanced ($0.15/vCPU-hr cluster-implied) is 67%, aligned with feature differential (Azure support, CMEK, 99.999% multi-region SLA); per-vCPU reading would have implied 233% premium which is implausible for the feature gap. (17) Pablo browser-validated cockroachlabs.com/pricing on 2026-05-05; tier names, prices, and PREVIEW status confirmed visually. Cluster-total interpretation accepted by Pablo; per-vCPU interpretation rejected. Cloud_entry_price = USD 438/month written with HIGH confidence given Pablo's direct validation. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 5: (18) feature_gating values for CockroachDB match MongoDB's record exactly across all 8 features (sso_saml, rbac_fine_grained, audit_logs, sla_support_tier, multi_region_ha all 'commercial'; white_label and api_rate_limits 'na'; advanced_analytics 'partial'). This reflects a structural similarity: both products gate the same canonical enterprise features behind a commercial license relationship while leaving basic capabilities (built-in roles, basic SQL/aggregation) accessible. Methodologically, the November 2024 CSL transition (commit c0274df, effective v24.3 release 2024-11-18) did NOT change the gating values for CockroachDB — it changed the access mechanism (revenue threshold of USD 10M + License Key issuance for the free Enterprise tier, instead of the prior BSL Core-vs-CCL Enterprise binary) but the underlying feature-level capabilities remain commercial-relationship-gated. This stability is methodologically meaningful for the Atlas: Hypothesis 1 testing (license migration patterns) is enriched by the observation that the license model can change dramatically while the open-core feature_gating structure remains consistent. Decisions (rbac_fine_grained=commercial vs partial; multi_region_ha=commercial vs partial) follow MongoDB precedent: the field name semantics override the broader feature interpretation (e.g., 'rbac_fine_grained' measures the fine-grained capability gate, not whether ANY RBAC exists). Documented to ensure cross-record consistency across the Atlas. Optional Pablo browser-validations deferred to Day-2 sanity if needed: (a) confirm CDC remains Enterprise-gated post-CSL via cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/change-data-capture-overview, (b) confirm pgvector availability in CSL Free self-hosted, (c) confirm multi-region survival goals (REGION_FAILURE / ZONE_FAILURE) all Enterprise. None of these would change the gating values themselves; they would refine verification_method wording. KEY METHODOLOGY DECISIONS FOR PHASE 6: (19) github velocity stats collected on a single day (2026-05-05) per Atlas Definition of Done item 7 — no stitching across days. Methodology reused from MongoDB Phase 6 walkthrough: (a) stars=32130 from gh api repos endpoint (live count). (b) contributors_total=831 from web sidebar count (manual peek by Pablo); the gh api repos/cockroachdb/cockroach/contributors paginated endpoint groups by email and only links the first 500 emails to GitHub user identities, so it underestimates for repos this size — sidebar number is more accurate per METHODOLOGY 7.15. (c) contributors_l12m=53 from stats/contributors top-100 endpoint, counting those with non-zero commits in the last 52 weeks; this is a LOWER BOUND because the endpoint caps at top-100 by total contribution count per METHODOLOGY 7.15. Typical OSS Pareto distribution suggests >95% capture but not exact. (d) commits_l12m=13843 from sum of weekly stats/commit_activity over 52 weeks (~266 commits/week average, ~38/day — consistent with sustained large-team engineering activity). (e) releases_l12m=133 from DETERMINISTIC exhaustive GraphQL pagination of refs(refPrefix:'refs/tags/') with annotated-tag handling per METHODOLOGY 7.13 (target.__typename + ... on Tag fragment) and exhaustive scan per METHODOLOGY 7.14 (no early-break, accept TAG_COMMIT_DATE DESC sort wobble). Total tags ever = 932 per same scan. (f) latest_release_date=2026-04-21 and latest_release_version=v24.1.28 from atom feed at github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/releases.atom (LTS patch); the chronologically-latest tag wins regardless of which release branch — same convention as MongoDB. (20) Cross-record velocity observation: CockroachDB vs MongoDB exhibit similar 'high velocity' profiles but with structurally different composition. CockroachDB ships 133 releases in L12M against only 932 lifetime tags (14% of all-time tags happened in L12M — strong recency signal); MongoDB ships 97 releases L12M against 1484 lifetime tags (6.5% in L12M). The CockroachDB pattern reflects multi-branch parallel maintenance (23.2.x + 24.1.x + 24.3.x + 25.2.x + 25.4.x + 26.1.x + 26.2.x = 7 active release branches simultaneously, each receiving rapid patch backports) on a younger codebase (~10 years vs MongoDB's ~18 years). Both qualify as high-velocity for any future velocity_tier classification but the patterns are distinct: CockroachDB = recent intensity / many parallel branches; MongoDB = longevity / fewer-branch deeper history. This nuance is methodologically meaningful for the Atlas because raw 'releases_l12m' alone hides composition differences that may matter for Hypothesis 6 (development velocity vs commercial outcome correlation). (21) GitHub stats collection methodology gotcha NOT re-encountered: MongoDB walkthrough exposed that initial GraphQL query for releases_l12m without '... on Tag {tagger { date }}' fragment returned false 0 because mongodb/mongo uses annotated tags. CockroachDB walkthrough RE-USED the corrected GraphQL query template developed during MongoDB and produced clean deterministic counts on first attempt (no 0 → 95 → 91 → 97 oscillation as MongoDB experienced). Open TODO post-Free-Preview: extend scripts/github_stats.sh with the corrected query for ergonomic re-use across the remaining 28 records.
